# DIM-T2I Download Instruction

**DIM-T2I** is a large-scale image-text dataset originally consisting of **~14M images** curated from publicly available sources, where every image is paired with an **ultra-long, detailed caption** generated by powerful VLMs to foster complex instruction comprehension abilities.

Due to legal and licensing constraints of the underlying sources, we currently release **~6M images** to the public, and reserve the possibility of releasing the remaining data in the future. For users seeking higher-quality captions, we recommend re-captioning the images with more recent VLMs such as [Qwen3.6-35B-A3B](https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B), following the prompts provided in the Appendix of [our paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.01986).

To help users fully reproduce the results reported in our paper, we have released our fully-trained models and the complete editing dataset:

- **DIM-4.6B-T2I**: 🤗[`stdKonjac/DIM-4.6B-T2I`](https://huggingface.co/stdKonjac/DIM-4.6B-T2I)
- **DIM-4.6B-Edit**: 🤗[`stdKonjac/DIM-4.6B-Edit`](https://huggingface.co/stdKonjac/DIM-4.6B-Edit)
- **DIM-Edit**: 🤗[`stdKonjac/DIM-Edit`](https://huggingface.co/datasets/stdKonjac/DIM-Edit)

This document describes how to obtain the source images for each sub-source of the released DIM-T2I data.

---

## 🚫 Image Redistribution & Legal Notice

> [!IMPORTANT]
> Due to legal and licensing restrictions of the underlying datasets, **DIM-T2I does not host or redistribute any source images**. We release **only**:
> - 📝 **Long, detailed captions** produced by us.
> - 🔗 **Per-sample metadata** (`image_path` field) that links each caption back to its original image in the upstream repository.
>
> To assemble the full dataset, you must **download the images yourself** from each upstream source listed below, then join them with our captions via the `image_path` key.
>
> All source images remain the property of their original creators and are governed by the licenses of their respective upstream datasets. **Users are solely responsible for complying with the terms of service and licensing agreements of each original data source.** By using DIM-T2I, you agree to honor these upstream obligations.

---

## 📥 Caption Metadata Download

Our captions and per-sample metadata are hosted at 🤗[`stdKonjac/DIM-T2I`](https://huggingface.co/datasets/stdKonjac/DIM-T2I). Download them with the `hf` CLI:

```bash
# 1. Install the huggingface_hub library (>= 0.32.0 for hf_xet support)
pip install -U huggingface_hub

# 2. Log in with your Hugging Face account token
hf auth login

# 3. Download the metadata
hf download stdKonjac/DIM-T2I --repo-type dataset --local-dir ./DIM-T2I
```

After downloading, the `DIM-T2I/` directory contains one JSONL file per upstream sub-source:

```
DIM-T2I/
├── allava_laion.jsonl                  # ALLaVA-4V (laion)
├── allava_vflan.jsonl                  # ALLaVA-4V (vflan)
├── allenai_pixmo_docs_charts.jsonl     # PixMo-Docs (charts)
├── allenai_pixmo_docs_diagrams.jsonl   # PixMo-Docs (diagrams)
├── allenai_pixmo_docs_other.jsonl      # PixMo-Docs (other)
├── allenai_pixmo_docs_tables.jsonl     # PixMo-Docs (tables)
├── arxivqa.jsonl                       # ArxivQA
├── blip3.jsonl                         # BLIP3-KALE
├── caps_fusion.jsonl                   # CapsFusion-120M
├── chartqa.jsonl                       # ChartQA
├── evqa.jsonl                          # Encyclopedic VQA
├── lmms-lab_LLaVA-ReCap-CC3M.jsonl     # LLaVA-ReCap-CC3M
├── sharegpt4o.jsonl                    # ShareGPT-4o
├── textocr-gpt4v.jsonl                 # TextOCR-GPT4V
├── textvqa.jsonl                       # TextVQA
├── ureader-instruction-1.0.jsonl       # UReader
└── vision-flan.jsonl                   # Vision-Flan
```

Each line of every JSONL is a JSON object containing the long, detailed caption and an `image_path` field.

> [!NOTE]
> `image_path` is **not a local file path**. It is the upstream key (e.g., `uid`, `image_id`, `id`, `image`, `filename`) used to retrieve the corresponding image from each source dataset. The exact matching rule per sub-source is documented in [Per-Source Download Instructions](#-per-source-download-instructions).

---

## 📊 Sub-Source Overview

| #  | Sub-source                              | Samples       | Upstream Host |
|---:|-----------------------------------------|--------------:|---------------|
| 1  | [BLIP3-KALE](#blip3-kale)               |     3,220,896 | 🤗 Hugging Face |
| 2  | [CapsFusion-120M](#capsfusion-120m)     |     1,554,581 | 🤗 Hugging Face (URLs) |
| 3  | [LLaVA-ReCap-CC3M](#llava-recap-cc3m)   |     1,123,373 | 🤗 Hugging Face |
| 4  | [PixMo-Docs](#pixmo-docs)               |       109,142 | 🤗 Hugging Face |
| 5  | [UReader](#ureader)                     |       105,365 | 🤗 Hugging Face |
| 6  | [ALLaVA-4V](#allava-4v)                 |        46,610 | 🤗 Hugging Face |
| 7  | [Vision-Flan](#vision-flan)             |        41,724 | 🤗 Hugging Face |
| 8  | [TextOCR-GPT4V](#textocr-gpt4v)         |        22,010 | 🤗 Hugging Face |
| 9  | [ArxivQA](#arxivqa)                     |        19,722 | 🤗 Hugging Face |
| 10 | [TextVQA](#textvqa)                     |        19,173 | Official website |
| 11 | [ShareGPT-4o](#sharegpt-4o)             |        17,411 | 🤗 Hugging Face |
| 12 | [ChartQA](#chartqa)                     |        15,389 | 🤗 Hugging Face |
| 13 | [Encyclopedic VQA](#encyclopedic-vqa)   |        14,522 | Google Landmarks v2 |
|    | **Total**                               | **~6.31M**    |               |

---

## 🛠️ General Workflow

Once you have the JSONL metadata, the procedure for every sub-source is the same:

1. **Download the source images** from the upstream repository linked below.
2. **Locate each image** by matching the `image_path` field in our JSONL against the upstream key (column name varies per source, see each section).
3. **Pair** the image with our long caption to obtain the final training sample.

---

## 📦 Per-Source Download Instructions

### BLIP3-KALE

**Metadata**
- `blip3.jsonl` (3,220,896 samples)

**Steps**
1. Download images from the [official Hugging Face repo](https://huggingface.co/datasets/Salesforce/blip3-kale).
2. Match `image_path` against the `uid` field of the HF dataset (subset `core`).

---

### CapsFusion-120M

**Metadata**
- `caps_fusion.jsonl` (1,554,581 samples)

**Steps**
1. We sample 1.5M images from the [official Hugging Face repo](https://huggingface.co/datasets/BAAI/CapsFusion-120M).
2. In `caps_fusion.jsonl`, the `image_path` field is itself an `image_url`. You can download the image directly from that URL, e.g.:
   ```
   https://www.mastercheffafoodphotography.it/wp-content/uploads/2018/09/insalata-fantasia-2-960x720.jpg
   ```
   You may also retrieve additional captions from the upstream repo.

---

### LLaVA-ReCap-CC3M

**Metadata**
- `lmms-lab_LLaVA-ReCap-CC3M.jsonl` (1,123,373 samples)

**Steps**
1. Download images from the [official Hugging Face repo](https://huggingface.co/datasets/lmms-lab/LLaVA-ReCap-CC3M).
2. Match `image_path` against the `id` field of the HF dataset (split `train`).

---

### PixMo-Docs

**Metadata**
- `allenai_pixmo_docs_charts.jsonl` (34,047 samples)
- `allenai_pixmo_docs_diagrams.jsonl` (9,754 samples)
- `allenai_pixmo_docs_other.jsonl` (38,872 samples)
- `allenai_pixmo_docs_tables.jsonl` (26,469 samples)

**Steps**
1. Download images from the [official Hugging Face repo](https://huggingface.co/datasets/allenai/pixmo-docs).
2. Match `image_path` in each JSONL against the `image_id` field of the HF dataset (subsets `charts`, `diagrams`, `other`, `tables`).

---

### UReader

**Metadata**
- `ureader-instruction-1.0.jsonl` (105,365 samples)

**Steps**
1. Download images from the [official Hugging Face repo](https://huggingface.co/datasets/Mizukiluke/ureader-instruction-1.0).
2. Match `image_path` against the `__key__` field of the HF dataset (split `train`), e.g. `DUE_Benchmark/TabFact/pngs/2-10979230-5`.

---

### ALLaVA-4V

**Metadata**
- `allava_laion.jsonl` (42,256 samples)
- `allava_vflan.jsonl` (4,354 samples)

**Steps**
1. Download images from the [official Hugging Face repo](https://huggingface.co/datasets/FreedomIntelligence/ALLaVA-4V).
2. Match `image_path` in our JSONL against the `image` field of the HF dataset (subsets `allava_laion` and `allava_vflan`).

---

### Vision-Flan

**Metadata**
- `vision-flan.jsonl` (41,724 samples)

**Steps**
1. Download images from the [official Hugging Face repo](https://huggingface.co/datasets/Vision-Flan/vision-flan_191-task_1k).
2. Match `image_path` against the `image` field of the HF dataset (split `train`), e.g. `PlotQA+visual_question_answering_480_4643.png`.

---

### TextOCR-GPT4V

**Metadata**
- `textocr-gpt4v.jsonl` (22,010 samples)

**Steps**
1. Download images from the [official Hugging Face repo](https://huggingface.co/datasets/jimmycarter/textocr-gpt4v).
2. Match `image_path` against the `filename` field of the HF dataset (split `train`), e.g. `c6131d9fe039fe7d.jpg`.

---

### ArxivQA

**Metadata**
- `arxivqa.jsonl` (19,722 samples)

**Steps**
1. Download images from the [official Hugging Face repo](https://huggingface.co/datasets/MMInstruction/ArxivQA).
2. Match `image_path` against the `image` field of the HF dataset (split `train`).

---

### TextVQA

**Metadata**
- `textvqa.jsonl` (19,173 samples)

**Steps**
1. Download [`train_val_images.zip`](https://dl.fbaipublicfiles.com/textvqa/images/train_val_images.zip) from the [official website](https://textvqa.org/dataset/).
2. Unzip to obtain the `train_images/` folder.
3. Use `image_path` to locate images, e.g. `train_images/bec6446e0ef5ef4d.jpg`.

---

### ShareGPT-4o

**Metadata**
- `sharegpt4o.jsonl` (17,411 samples)

**Steps**
1. Download [`images.zip`](https://huggingface.co/datasets/OpenGVLab/ShareGPT-4o/resolve/main/images.zip?download=true) from the [official Hugging Face repo](https://huggingface.co/datasets/OpenGVLab/ShareGPT-4o).
2. Unzip and navigate into the innermost folder `4o/image/`.
3. Match `image_path` against filenames in that folder, e.g. `25295.jpg`.

---

### ChartQA

**Metadata**
- `chartqa.jsonl` (15,389 samples)

**Steps**
1. Download [`ChartQA Dataset.zip`](https://huggingface.co/datasets/ahmed-masry/ChartQA/resolve/main/ChartQA%20Dataset.zip?download=true) from the [official Hugging Face repo](https://huggingface.co/datasets/ahmed-masry/ChartQA).
2. Unzip to obtain the `ChartQA Dataset/` directory.
3. Use the `image_path` field in `chartqa.jsonl` to locate images, e.g. `ChartQA Dataset/train/png/two_col_60728.png`.

---

### Encyclopedic VQA

**Metadata**
- `evqa.jsonl` (14,522 samples)

**Steps**
1. We use the landmark images from [Encyclopedic VQA (EVQA)](https://github.com/google-research/google-research/tree/master/encyclopedic_vqa), which are originally sourced from the [Google Landmarks Dataset v2](https://github.com/cvdfoundation/google-landmark).
2. Download the labels file [`train.csv`](https://s3.amazonaws.com/google-landmark/metadata/train.csv).
3. Match `image_path` against the `id` column in `train.csv`, then download each image from the corresponding `url` column.

---
